items.text: Effective May 15, the recycling bins near the Student Union will be relocated to the east side of Anderson Hall. A $5 fee will be charged for any items left at the old location after 5:00 PM. Please direct questions to the Facilities Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must be paid if items are left at the old recycling location after 5:00 PM?
questions.explanation: The notice states that a $5 fee will be charged for any items left at the old location after 5:00 PM, so option A is correct.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the recycling bin relocation?

options.A: The bins will be removed permanently from the campus.
options.B: The change will cost the university additional funds.
options.C: Students are discouraged from using the old location after the deadline.
options.D: The new location will offer more recycling services.

questions.explanation: The notice implies that leaving items at the old location after the deadline will incur a fee, so students are discouraged from doing so, making option C correct.

items.text: The water fountain on the third floor of the library will be closed for maintenance from March 3 to March 7. During this period, please use the fountain on the second floor. For assistance, contact the building manager at extension 245.

questions.stem: According to the notice, which water fountain is available for use during the maintenance period?

options.A: The one on the third floor
options.C: The one on the second floor

questions.explanation: The notice states to use the fountain on the second floor while the third-floor fountain is closed, so option C is correct.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the maintenance work?

options.A: It will be completed before March 3.
options.B: It will require the library to close temporarily.
options.C: It will affect all water fountains in the building.
options.D: It is scheduled to last for a limited time only.

questions.explanation: The notice specifies the maintenance from March 3 to March 7, indicating it is a temporary closure, so option D is correct.

items.text: Hi Jamie, your interlibrary loan book, "Ecology of the Amazon," has arrived. Please pick it up at the circulation desk by this Friday, March 10, or a $1 fee will apply. Best, Ms. Chen

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Jamie do by this Friday?

options.D: Retrieve the book from the circulation desk

questions.explanation: The email states Jamie should pick up the book at the circulation desk by Friday, so option D is correct.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the interlibrary loan process?

options.A: The book was ordered from a nearby library.
options.B: A fee may be charged if the book is not collected on time.
options.C: The book is available for an extended loan period.
options.D: The pickup location is the main library entrance.

questions.explanation: The email mentions a $1 fee will apply if the book is not picked up by Friday, so a late collection incurs a charge, making option B correct.
